subtitle: A riverbank revelation and the mystery of a mirror-bright queen of stone

panels.shot: epic low-angle wide of towering sculpture against darkened museum hall
panels.action: The Didarganj Yakshi stands tall in a dim gallery, fly-whisk raised, her polished Chunar sandstone surface catching a single shaft of light like burnished bronze.

dialogue.text: In the age of empires and rivers, the Didarganj Yakshi rose from mud and memory, her shining stone defying time and refusing a simple date.

i18n.hi: साम्राज्यों और नदियों के दौर में, कीचड़ और यादों से दीदारगंज यक्षी उठी, उसका चमकता पत्थर समय को चुनौती देता, तारीख से इंकार करता।

panels.shot: serene wide shot of the Ganga near Patna at dawn
panels.action: Mist curls over the Ganga near Didarganj Kadam Rasual, boats moored by the muddy bank, palm trees and village huts faint against a pale October sky.

dialogue.text: On an October morning in 1917, the Ganga flowed past Didarganj as it had for ages, carrying silt, stories, and secrets buried on her banks.

i18n.hi: अक्टूबर 1917 की एक सुबह, गंगा दीदारगंज के पास सदियों की तरह बह रही थी, सिल्ट, किस्से और किनारे में दबे राज़ बहाती हुई।

panels.shot: medium shot of village men working on the riverbank
panels.action: Barefoot villagers dig and pry at the eroding riverbank, one man pausing in surprise as his tool strikes something hard beneath the wet clay.

dialogue.text: Villagers cutting into the bank found stone where they expected only earth, their spades ringing against a hidden form.

i18n.hi: तट काटते गाँववालों को मिट्टी की जगह पत्थर मिला, फावड़े छिपी हुई मूर्ति से टकरा कर छनक उठे।

panels.shot: dramatic low-angle as the upper torso of the Yakshi emerges from mud
panels.action: Half-buried in the slick mud, the upper body of the Didarganj Yakshi tilts out from the bank, her calm face and smooth shoulder smeared with river clay yet unmistakably sculpted.

dialogue.text: As hands scraped away mud, a woman of stone appeared—tall, serene, and shining even through the clay that veiled her.

i18n.hi: जैसे-जैसे हाथों ने कीचड़ हटाया, पत्थर की एक स्त्री उभरी—लंबी, शांत, और मिट्टी की परत के बीच भी चमकती हुई।

panels.shot: slow push-in on the Yakshi’s polished surface catching light amid mud
panels.action: A close view reveals patches where the mud has been wiped, exposing an astonishing mirror-like polish on the Yakshi’s torso that reflects sky and faces in soft distortion.

dialogue.text: Beneath the river’s stain lay Chunar sandstone worked to a gleam so deep it threw back light like still water.

i18n.hi: नदी के दाग़ों के नीचे चुनार पत्थर था, इतना घिसा-पिटा कि स्थिर पानी की तरह रोशनी लौटा रहा था।

panels.shot: frontal medium shot showing full figure with fly-whisk
panels.action: The Didarganj Yakshi stands fully upright now, propped by ropes, her right hand elegantly holding a long, curved chauri, her body firmly planted, head slightly tilted in quiet authority.

dialogue.text: Life-sized, bearing a fly-whisk, she seemed a celestial attendant suddenly returned to human sight from some older court.

i18n.hi: जीवन-आकार, चंवर थामे, वो किसी स्वर्गिक परिचारिका सी लगी, जैसे किसी प्राचीन दरबार से अचानक मानव आँखों के सामने लौट आई हो।

panels.shot: museum-style side profile showing ornaments and forelock
panels.action: Side profile reveals the Yakshi’s thick forelock falling over the forehead, heavy necklaces, girdle, and subtle drapery lines across her polished form, all carved with deliberate rhythm.

dialogue.text: Her forelock, her ornaments, the faint sweep of cloth across her body—every detail carried the touch of a sculptor thinking in style, not only in shine.

i18n.hi: उसकी लट, गहने, और शरीर पर कपड़े की हल्की लहर—हर सूक्ष्म रेखा में एक मूर्तिकार का अंदाज़ झलकता था, सिर्फ चमक नहीं।

panels.shot: wide interior shot of early Patna Museum hall
panels.action: Inside the early 20th-century Patna Museum, the Yakshi stands on a stone pedestal, surrounded by banners and modest showcases, visitors craning their necks to take in her height and polish.

dialogue.text: Carried from the riverbank to Patna Museum, she entered the new world of labels and catalogues, an ancient figure now under modern eyes.

i18n.hi: नदी किनारे से उठाकर पटना म्यूज़ियम लाई गई, वो अब लेबल और कैटलॉग की दुनिया में दाख़िल हुई—पुरातन देह पर आधुनिक नज़रें टिक गईं।

panels.shot: scholarly medium shot with curator pointing at Yakshi
panels.action: A museum curator stands beside the Yakshi, gesturing toward her gleaming surface, as a simple display card by the pedestal confidently names her as Mauryan-era art.

dialogue.text: Her mirror polish, famed on the pillars of Ashoka, led many to mark her as Mauryan glory—an imperial lady of the third century BCE.

i18n.hi: उसका आईने जैसा पालिश, जो अशोक के स्तंभों पर मशहूर था, कई लोगों को उसे मौर्य वैभव मानने पर उकसाता—तीसरी सदी ईसा-पूर्व की शाही नारी।

panels.shot: slow push-in on Yakshi’s head as pages of a scholarly book dissolve around
panels.action: The Yakshi’s serene face is overlaid by transparent pages from James C. Harle’s book, diagrams and photos of yakshi figures from different periods surrounding her image.

dialogue.text: Yet art historians like James C. Harle looked again, weighing not only her shine, but her hair, jewelry, and drapery against other early historic yakshis.

i18n.hi: पर जेम्स सी. हार्ल जैसे कला-इतिहासकारों ने फिर देखा, सिर्फ चमक नहीं, उसके बाल, गहने और वस्त्र को अन्य प्राचीन यक्षियों से तौला।

panels.shot: analytical montage: comparative silhouettes of other yakshi sculptures
panels.action: In a comparative spread, silhouettes of the Didarganj Yakshi stand beside Kushan-period yakshi figures; lines and arrows highlight shared features of forelock, stance, and garment folds.

dialogue.text: Her heavy forelock, certain ornaments, and the way cloth clings and falls seemed closer to yakshi figures from the Kushan centuries, long after the Mauryas fell.

i18n.hi: उसकी भारी लटें, कुछ ख़ास आभूषण, और कपड़े का चिपकना-बहना, मौर्यों के बाद के कुशाण युग की यक्षियों के ज़्यादा करीब लगे।

panels.shot: medium shot of Yakshi under changing date labels
panels.action: The Yakshi stands unchanged while museum labels around her subtly shift across time, one reading “Mauryan,” another “early historic,” and another “1st–2nd century CE?” with a cautious question mark.

dialogue.text: Scholars argued, dates moved. Chunar sandstone could be polished for generations. Shine alone could not pin her to Ashoka’s court.

i18n.hi: विद्वान बहस करते रहे, तारीखें सरकती रहीं। चुनार पत्थर पीढ़ियों तक चमकाया जा सकता था। सिर्फ चमक से उसे अशोक के दरबार में नहीं बाँधा जा सकता।

panels.shot: close-up of Yakshi’s polished torso beside a polished pillar fragment
panels.action: Her gleaming torso is shown beside a fragment of a polished stone pillar, their surfaces equally bright, a small note indicating that the technique endured beyond Mauryan rule.

dialogue.text: The famed Mauryan polish lived on in later workshops. The Yakshi’s gleam spoke of skill and patronage, but not of a single royal date.

i18n.hi: मौर्यकालीन पालिश बाद की कार्यशालाओं में भी जिंदा रही। यक्षी की चमक कौशल और सरपरस्ती की गवाह थी, किसी एक शाही दिन की नहीं।

panels.shot: somber wide shot of Yakshi in foreign gallery with nose subtly chipped
panels.action: Displayed far from India in a bright international gallery, the Yakshi’s face shows a small, noticeable damage at the nose, visitors admiring her unaware of the fresh loss.

dialogue.text: On distant travels in the 1980s, her nose was damaged, a reminder that fragile stone carries not just beauty, but the risk of modern harm.

i18n.hi: दूर के दौरों में 1980 के दशक में उसकी नाक टूट गई—याद दिलाती कि नाज़ुक पत्थर सौंदर्य के साथ-साथ आधुनिक चोट भी ढोता है।

panels.shot: reverent wide shot of Bihar Museum gallery
panels.action: In the Bihar Museum at Patna, the Yakshi now stands in a spacious, well-lit early historic gallery, glass cases and careful signage respectfully framing her presence.

dialogue.text: Returned and rehoused at Bihar Museum, she stands within an early historic gallery, surrounded by objects that share her long, uncertain span of time.

i18n.hi: लौटकर बिहार म्यूज़ियम में बसेरा पाकर, वो आरंभिक ऐतिहासिक दीर्घा में खड़ी है, उन वस्तुओं से घिरी जो उसके जैसे ही लंबा, अनिश्चित समय साझा करती हैं।

panels.shot: close shot of a modern museum label near Yakshi
panels.action: A modern museum label beside her pedestal is legible enough to show cautious wording: “early historic,” “Mauryan or Kushan debated,” and references to recent scholarship.

dialogue.text: Now the caption steps back from certainty, marking her as early historic, her date debated between Mauryan and Kushan worlds.

i18n.hi: अब उसके नीचे की पट्टी यक़ीन से पीछे हटती है, उसे आरंभिक ऐतिहासिक कहती है—तारीख मौर्य और कुशाण दुनियाओं के बीच बहस में अटकी है।

panels.shot: medium shot of visitors observing Yakshi thoughtfully
panels.action: Students and elders stand quietly around the Yakshi, some reading, some simply gazing; her polished surface softly mirrors them, blending their faces with her ancient form.

dialogue.text: Visitors lean close, reading the questions, then look up at her, letting wonder sit beside doubt rather than forcing an easy answer.

i18n.hi: दर्शक झुककर सवाल पढ़ते हैं, फिर सिर उठाकर उसे देखते हैं, जहाँ हैरानी और शक साथ बैठते हैं, आसान जवाब थोपे बिना।

panels.shot: intimate close-up of Yakshi’s calm face
panels.action: An extreme close view of the Yakshi’s face shows her tranquil gaze and faint smile, the chip at the nose visible, yet the overall expression undisturbed by age or argument.

dialogue.text: You may not know when I was born, only that I endured.

i18n.hi: तुम्हें शायद मेरा जन्म नहीं पता, पर इतना जानो कि मैं टिक कर रही।

panels.shot: final wide shot of Yakshi silhouetted against timeline mural
panels.action: The Yakshi is silhouetted against a mural showing a long timeline from Mauryas to Kushans, her figure placed between eras, not pinned to either, bathed in gentle, even light.

dialogue.text: The Didarganj Yakshi stands between dates, teaching that honest history holds questions close, and that true wonder needs no simple, certain label.

i18n.hi: तारीखों के बीच खड़ी दीदारगंज यक्षी बताती है कि सच्चा इतिहास सवालों को पास रखता है, और असली विस्मय को किसी आसान, पक्के लेबल की ज़रूरत नहीं।

subtitle_i18n.en: A riverbank revelation and the mystery of a mirror-bright queen of stone
subtitle_i18n.hi: नदी किनारे की खोज और पत्थर की आईने-जैसी रानी का रहस्य
